title: Paper Library — Semantic Edges + Claims + Merges
description: LOCAL consumer schema (S1) — the Semantic Layer / brain-map atoms. Carries exactly the five things S0 found missing from the shared claims_index (multiple evidence refs per claim; edge lifecycle; reversible merge records; projection provenance) plus the claim/evidence/stance triple from the argument-mining finding. LOCAL ONLY (C8): NOT an amendment to claims_index. Promotion to the shared schema is gated on D5 (>=2 consumers exercise it independently AND >=1 cross-check survives divergent machinery). The relation vocabulary is drawn from gpt's plan (superset of ours).

claims.description: Brain-map claim atoms. Each is one statement grounded in >=1 passage (multiple evidence refs — the first S0 gap over claims_index).

evidence.description: MULTIPLE evidence refs per claim (S0 gap #1). Each points at a paper passage.

evidence_tier.description: Reuses the shared artifact_types evidence_axis vocabulary (alignment without amending it).

origin.description: extraction = pulled from a passage; inference = LLM-derived, not directly stated; manual = human-authored. Assertions without source evidence must be visibly labeled inference (S0 invariant).

model_version.description: Model+prompt version that produced a generated claim (adopted from gpt's plan — reproducibility).

owner.description: Manual-correction ownership (S0 gap #2). owner=human MUST NOT be overwritten by regeneration (S4 invariant).

edges.description: Typed semantic relations between claims and/or papers. Lifecycle + provenance are first-class (S0 gaps #3, #5).

rel.description: Typed relation vocabulary (superset from gpt's plan). Closed set, code-enforced.

lifecycle.description: Edge lifecycle (S0 gap #3). LLM emits proposed; human/second-pass confirms or rejects; a newer edge supersedes. Nothing enters projections as truth until confirmed.

superseded_by.description: If lifecycle=superseded, the edge_id that replaces it.

merges.description: REVERSIBLE merge records (S0 gap #4). A merge is a LINK, not a destructive rewrite: both original claims are retained; the merge can be undone. Records who/what decided, so identity decisions are auditable (the leak-doctrine applied to knowledge).

merged.description: claim_ids folded into canonical (retained, not deleted).

decided_by.description: How the identity decision was made (S6 decision-log axis).

reversible.description: Merges are always reversible by construction.
